steps.title: Identify all factors from the zeros
steps.content: The points $(-6, 0)$, $(7, 0)$, $(0, 0)$, and $(4, 0)$ are zeros of $f$. Therefore, $f(x)$ is divisible by the linear factors $x$, $(x-7)$, $(x+6)$, and $(x-4)$. We can write $$f(x) = x(x-4)(x-7)(x+6) \cdot q(x)$$ where $q(x)$ is some polynomial.

steps.title: Find which choice is a product of known factors
steps.content:
Any product of our known factors must also divide $f(x)$. Let's check each choice:
  • Choice A: $x^2 - 2x - 24 = (x-6)(x+4)$ requires factors $(x-6)$ and $(x+4)$, but these are not in our list.
  • Choice B: $x^2 - x + 42$ has discriminant $1 - 4(42) = -167 < 0$, so its roots are complex and don't match our real zeros.
  • Choice C: $x^2 - 11x - 28$ — if it factored from two of our zeros, they'd sum to 11 and multiply to $-28$. The zeros $7$ and $4$ sum to 11, but $(7)(4) = 28$, not $-28$.
  • Choice D: $x^2 - 7x = x(x-7)$ — both $x$ and $(x-7)$ are confirmed factors of $f$.
That matches choice D — D is correct.
